# Solutions: Continuous Integration in RAP pipeline

This notebook provides step-by-step solutions for implementing and testing continuous integration (CI) best practices in your RAP pipeline. Each solution matches the corresponding exercise notebook and includes instructions for checking your changes and expected results.

## Solution 1: Explore the CI configuration files

-   Open `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, and `DESCRIPTION` in your project root.
-   Review which hooks and tools are enabled for Python scripts and notebooks.
-   **Expected result:** You should see hooks for check-added-large-files, end-of-file-fixer and detect-secrets

## Solution 2: Detect secrets in your code

-   Add a fake secret to any Python file. For example:

    ``` python
    password = "add_your_fake_password_here"
    print(password)
    ```

-   Run:

    ``` cmd
    pre-commit run bandit --all-files
    ```

-   **How to check:** The hook will flag the secret in the output. Remove the secret and rerun the hook.

-   **Expected result:** No secrets detected after removal; hook passes.

## Solution 3: Add a new workflow that activates on push to any branch

-   Create a new workflow file in `.github/workflows/ci_test_coverage.yml` with the following content: \`\`\`yaml name: Test coverage CI (Any Branch)

on: \# Run this workflow when you push to any branch push: branches: [ "\*" ]

jobs: build: \# Use the latest Ubuntu operating system for the runner runs-on: ubuntu-latest \# This is a virtual machine provided by GitHub steps: \# Step 1: Download your repository code onto the runner - uses: [actions/checkout\@v4](mailto:actions/checkout@v4){.email} \# Step 2: Set up R version 4.4.0 on the runner - name: Set up R uses: [r-lib/actions/setup-r\@v2](mailto:r-lib/actions/setup-r@v2){.email} \# check for latest version with: r-version: '4.4.0' \# Step 3: Cache R packages to speed up future runs - name: Set R_LIBS_USER run: echo "R_LIBS_USER=\$(Rscript -e 'cat(.libPaths()[1])')" \>\> \$GITHUB_ENV - name: Cache R packages uses: [actions/cache\@v3](mailto:actions/cache@v3){.email} with: path: \${{ env.R_LIBS_USER }} key: ${{ runner.os }}-r-${{ hashFiles('\*\*/\*.lock') }} restore-keys: \| \${{ runner.os }}-r- \# Step 4: Install all required R packages listed in DESCRIPTION - name: Install dependencies uses: [r-lib/actions/setup-r-dependencies\@v2](mailto:r-lib/actions/setup-r-dependencies@v2){.email} with: extra-packages: any::devtools\
\# Step 5: Run unit tests using devtools - name: Run tests run: Rscript -e 'devtools::test()' \# Step 6: Generate coverage report and upload as artifact - name: Generate coverage report run: Rscript -e "cov <- covr::package_coverage(); print(covr::percent_coverage(cov)); print(covr::zero_coverage(cov))"

```         
  - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
    with:
      name: coverage-report
      path: coverage.html
```

\`\`\` - Update DESCRIPTION to include the packages covr, DT and htmltools:

Package: r.rap.demo Title: R RAP Demo Version: 0.0.1 [Authors\@R](mailto:Authors@R){.email}: person("Analysis for Action", email = "[analysisforaction\@ons.gov.uk](mailto:analysisforaction@ons.gov.uk){.email}", role = c("aut", "cre")) Description: Learn RAP principles through practical R exercises. This repo showcases best practices for Reproducible Analytical Pipelines, with descriptions of components and exercises to help users build confidence in useapplying RAP techniques. License: MIT Imports: dplyr, testthat, yaml, readr, ggplot2, covr, DT, htmltools

-   Commit and push your change to any branch.
-   Go to your repository on GitHub and click the **Actions** tab at the top of the page.
-   Look for the workflow run named "Test coverage CI (Any Branch)" in the list of workflow runs.
-   Click on the workflow run to view its status and logs.
-   **Expected result:** The workflow should run automatically on any branch push and show its status (success or failure) in the Actions tab. If there are errors, they will be shown in the logs and the workflow will fail until fixed.

This action will run any time you push code up to GitHub on any branch.

## Reflection

By following these solutions, you have implemented and tested continuous integration in your RAP pipeline. This ensures your code is reproducible, robust, and meets quality standards automatically.
